title: How to Build an Effective Deliverable‑Focused Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)
summary: Practical steps for creating a clear, hierarchical WBS that captures 100 % of the project scope, uses deliverable‑oriented nouns, and involves the team that will produce the work.
content:
Guidelines:
• Use nouns, not verbs, to keep focus on deliverables.
• Apply a hierarchical structure to show relationships.
• Build the WBS collaboratively with the people who will produce the deliverables.
• Ensure each level contains unique deliverables—no duplication.
• Make all scope elements explicit; do not rely on stakeholder assumptions.
• Follow the 100 % rule at every level of decomposition.
• Validate completeness by asking whether any project solution concept is missing or out‑of‑scope.
can_answer: What are the best practices for creating a work breakdown structure?
How can I avoid common mistakes when building a WBS?
warnings: Skipping the 100 % rule can lead to missing scope.
